title: Implement the human-confirmed destination registry

commands.name: Missing destination registry domain
commands.command: Focused destination unit, property, and integration suites
commands.expectedFailure: Unit and property suites could not import the destination registry; all four integration cases failed because its repositories did not exist

commands.expectedFailure: Seed 20260723 shrank the overbroad hostname generator to a.0; the invalid numeric terminal label is retained as a permanent rejection example

commands.expectedFailure: Both scenarios failed before the panel existed and all fourteen DET-01 browser steps were undefined

commands.name: Production browser acceptance and source-bound visual capture
Focused lint, types, migration evidence, and destination suites
commands.command: Destination-focused ESLint, core/web typecheck, domain evidence check, six Vitest files, and git diff whitespace validation

results.counterexample: a.0 is retained as an invalid-hostname example after the first generator shrank outside the valid DNS input domain

scenarios.scenario: An observed destination stays unknown without a human-confirmed entity mapping
The privacy officer confirms allowed and prohibited destinations from exact sources

visualEvidence.rationale: DET-01 adds visible UNKNOWN, ALLOWED, and PROHIBITED registry states. The controlled browser stories capture both desktop confirmation outcomes and the narrow unknown default from the running product at the source commit.

assets.altText: Desktop destination card for classroom-service.pactwire.test showing ALLOWED, fictional Northstar entity identity, named human reviewer, exact agreement version, recorder observation count, retained source count, and immutable version 2.
assets.caption: A fictional privacy officer confirmed the hostname-to-entity mapping and ALLOWED status from the exact stored destination schedule; version 1 remains the earlier UNKNOWN observation.

assets.altText: Desktop destination card for fixture-analytics.pactwire.test showing PROHIBITED, fictional Signal Quarry entity identity, named human reviewer, exact agreement version, recorder observation count, retained source count, and immutable version 2.
assets.caption: The same human-only evidence path records a PROHIBITED status for a second fictional destination under the selected exact agreement version.

assets.altText: Narrow destination card for unknown-destination.pactwire.test showing UNKNOWN, immutable version 1, one recorder observation, one retained source record, and direct text that no company or agreement status is assigned.
assets.caption: A deterministic observation creates only an UNKNOWN record; the responsive UI explicitly says that no entity mapping or agreement status has been inferred.

proofs.description: Canonical hostnames, UNKNOWN schema, model-author rejection, and unseen resolution examples
Seeded PROP-04 unseen and observed-hostname invariants across 500 generated cases
Human evidence review, exact agreement quote validation, stale-review serialization, PostgreSQL transaction, automation rejection, and immutable history
Signed HTTP observation, human review, history, and reviewer-role denial
Real browser and API proof that an observed unknown destination receives no inferred company or agreement status
Real browser and API proof for exact-source ALLOWED and PROHIBITED human reviews
Strict evidence/version schemas, canonical hashes, fail-closed resolver, services, and in-memory/PostgreSQL repositories
Append-only destination versions with human authority, exact agreement evidence, latest-source, payload, and immutable database guards
Responsive observation, human evidence review, current classification, and version-history interface
Accepted P0 source types and explicit model, recorder, human, and exact-agreement authority boundaries

knownLimitations: DET-01 resolves registry identity and agreement status only; DET-03 still owns whether observed evidence creates a bounded finding.
A human confirmation records the reviewer's decision and cited evidence; it does not independently prove real-world ownership, legal meaning, safety, compliance, or software approval.
Signed-agreement evidence is checked against stored immutable bytes; other accepted mapping sources retain a locator, hash, excerpt, actor, and time but P0 does not ingest their full source objects.
The browser fixture uses ephemeral in-memory storage while PostgreSQL persistence and database guards are exercised separately in integration tests.
The task remains IN_PROGRESS until a clean GitHub Actions run succeeds; local deterministic gates and source-bound browser evidence are green.
